Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Write up of my homebrew CPU build 209 upvotes, 40 comments
  2. Show HN: Sub-millisecond VM sandboxes using CoW memory forking 278 upvotes, 65 comments
  3. Book: The Emerging Science of Machine Learning Benchmarks 12 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. Using calculus to do number theory 78 upvotes, 15 comments
  5. OpenRocket 163 upvotes, 39 comments
  6. Nightingale – open-source karaoke app that works with any song on your computer 412 upvotes, 117 comments
  7. Snowflake AI Escapes Sandbox and Executes Malware 179 upvotes, 53 comments
  8. Celebrating Tony Hoare's mark on computer science 108 upvotes, 29 comments
  9. 2025 Turing award given for quantum information science 48 upvotes, 11 comments
  10. The pleasures of poor product design 232 upvotes, 81 comments
  11. Wander – A tiny, decentralised tool (just 2 files) to explore the small web 56 upvotes, 16 comments
  12. Federal Cyber Experts Called Microsoft's Cloud "A Pile of Shit", yet Approved It 357 upvotes, 151 comments
  13. EU Inc.: A new harmonised corporate legal regime 29 upvotes, 7 comments
  14. Show HN: Hacker News archive (47M+ items, 11.6GB) as Parquet, updated every 5m 146 upvotes, 63 comments
  15. Rob Pike's Rules of Programming (1989) 697 upvotes, 366 comments
  16. Google Engineers Launch "Sashiko" for Agentic AI Code Review of the Linux Kernel 62 upvotes, 25 comments
  17. CVE-2026-3888: Important Snap Flaw Enables Local Privilege Escalation to Root 33 upvotes, 11 comments
  18. Get Shit Done: A meta-prompting, context engineering and spec-driven dev system 421 upvotes, 236 comments
  19. Death to Scroll Fade 276 upvotes, 150 comments
  20. Python 3.15's JIT is now back on track 461 upvotes, 279 comments
  21. A ngrok-style secure tunnel server written in Rust and Open Source 49 upvotes, 21 comments
  22. Machine Payments Protocol (MPP) 98 upvotes, 50 comments
  23. Restoring the first recording of computer music (2018) 24 upvotes, 8 comments
  24. Show HN: Playing LongTurn FreeCiv with Friends 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  25. Wanter – A tiny, decentralised tool to explore the small web 44 upvotes, 26 comments
  26. Nvidia NemoClaw 138 upvotes, 103 comments
  27. Show HN: Tmux-IDE, OSS agent-first terminal IDE 25 upvotes, 14 comments
  28. Show HN: Will my flight have Starlink? 79 upvotes, 62 comments
  29. AI coding is gambling 195 upvotes, 216 comments
  30. Ndea (YC W26) is hiring a symbolic RL search guidance lead 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 4:00am, 19 March 2026. Source here.
